--- title: Node Classification subtitle: Classifying and snapping WFRC network nodes to physical centerline endpoints description: WFRC MasterNet node conflation — snapping abstract model nodes to UGRC/UDOT physical centerlines author: - name: Pukar Bhandari email: pukar.bhandari@wfrc.utah.gov affiliation: - name: Wasatch Front Regional Council url: "https://wfrc.utah.gov/" date: "2026-04-03" --- ## Setup ### Imports ::: {#4165e919 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import sys from pathlib import Path import folium import geopandas as gpd import gtfs_kit as gk import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.ticker as mticker import numpy as np import pandas as pd import shapely from shapely.geometry import LineString from shapely.strtree import STRtree sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd())) from _src.node_utils import ( nodes_on, count_links, count_neighbors, assign_node_directions, assign_endpoint_directions, snap_nodes, snap_transit, snap_cc_nodes, ) ``` ::: ### Configuration ::: {#a9a752b1 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # ── Filepaths ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PATHS = { "in_centerlines": Path("_data/raw/RCL2TDMnew.gdb.zip"), "in_network_links": Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet/WFv1000_MasterNet_20250821 - Link.shp"), "in_network_nodes": Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet/WFv1000_MasterNet_20250821 - Node.shp"), "in_gtfs": Path("_data/raw/uta_gtfs.zip"), "out_node_classified": Path("_data/processed/nodes_classified.gpkg"), "out_node_snapped": Path("_data/processed/nodes_snapped.gpkg"), "out_debug": Path("_data/processed/debug_snapping.gpkg"), } # ── CRS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── CRS_UTM = "EPSG:26912" # NAD83 UTM Zone 12N — Utah (metric) CRS_WGS = "EPSG:4326" # WGS84 — required by Folium ``` ::: --- ## Load Data ### Roadway Centerlines ::: {#a6918278 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # 1. Prepare the Path # We use .as_posix() to handle slashes correctly regardless of OS # gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}" gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines'].as_posix()}" # 2. View the actual layers within the gdb gpd.list_layers(gdb_path) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
name geometry_type
0 RCL2TDMGeometry MultiLineString
1 RCLWasatchFrontAll MultiLineString
``` ::: ::: ::: {#7f9cf129 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} COUNTY_FIPS = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"] county_filter = "(" + ", ".join(f"'{f}'" for f in COUNTY_FIPS) + ")" gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file( gdb_path, # f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}", layer="RCLWasatchFrontAll", where=f"(DOT_FCLASS IN ('Interstate', 'Local', 'Major Collector', 'Minor Arterial', 'Minor Collector', 'Other Freeway', 'Principal Arterial') OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%C%') AND CARTOCODE <> '99' AND (COUNTY_L IN {county_filter} OR COUNTY_R IN {county_filter})", ).to_crs(CRS_UTM) # Strip trailing whitespace from categorical fields (source-data artefact) for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]: if col in gdf_centerlines.columns: gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip() print(f"Centerlines: {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Centerlines: 25,358 features | CRS: EPSG:26912 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#ef5d1bd6 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore() ``` ::: ### Network Links and Nodes ::: {#22cb916a .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Active 2027 links, excluding HOV / Managed Lane / Managed Lane Access (FT 37-39). # These are filtered at load time so they never inflate LinkCount, contribute to # the direction cascade, or generate spurious "surface" node classifications. gdf_links = gpd.read_file( PATHS["in_network_links"], where="LN_2027 > 0 AND FT_2027 NOT IN (1, 37, 38, 39)", ).to_crs(CRS_UTM) gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_network_nodes"]).to_crs(CRS_UTM) print(f"Links: {len(gdf_links):,} (LN_2027 > 0, FT not in 1/37/38/39)") print(f"Nodes: {len(gdf_nodes):,}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Links: 27,993 (LN_2027 > 0, FT not in 1/37/38/39) Nodes: 21,921 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#ba5e0566 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} gdf_links.head(1000).explore() gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore() ``` ::: ### GTFS Rail Stops Filter GTFS stops to Light Rail (TRAX, route_type 0) and Commuter Rail (FrontRunner, route_type 2) only, walking the routes → trips → stop_times chain. ::: {#5513989a .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} feed = gk.read_feed(PATHS["in_gtfs"], dist_units="km") gdf_stops = gk.get_stops(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM) gdf_shapes = gk.get_shapes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM) gdf_routes = gk.get_routes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM) RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES = {0, 2} # 0 = Light Rail (TRAX), 2 = Commuter Rail (FrontRunner) rail_route_ids = feed.routes.loc[feed.routes["route_type"].isin(RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES), "route_id"] rail_trip_ids = feed.trips.loc[feed.trips["route_id"].isin(rail_route_ids), "trip_id"] rail_stop_ids = feed.stop_times.loc[feed.stop_times["trip_id"].isin(rail_trip_ids), "stop_id"].unique() gdf_rail_stops = gdf_stops[gdf_stops["stop_id"].isin(rail_stop_ids)].copy() print(f"Rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops):,} of {len(gdf_stops):,} total stops") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Rail stops: 130 of 5,499 total stops ``` ::: ::: ::: {#7fc944ba .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} gdf_rail_stops.explore() ``` ::: --- ## Part A — Origin Processing (Network Nodes) ### A1 — Functional Type Flags `nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, ft_codes)` returns True for any node whose N appears as endpoint A or B of at least one link whose FT code is in `ft_codes`. Flags are **non-exclusive** — a node touching both a freeway and a ramp link will be True for both `Freeway` and `Ramp`. Mutual exclusion is enforced later in the topology cascade (§A3). ::: {#ee56a8cd .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} # ── Three mutually exclusive TDM link classes (2027 network year) ───────── # # FT_2027 code reference # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Class 1 — Freeway Mainline (FW_CODES): # 12-15 Expressway (limited-access, divided) # 22-26 MM freeway (posted 55-75 mph, MM series) # 32-36 Freeway (posted 55-75 mph, Fwy series) # 40 Express / managed-access freeway # FT=2 AND DIRECTION=1: directional Principal Arterial links representing # physically separated roadways (e.g. divided expressways coded as PA). # # Class 2 — Ramps and CDs (RAMP_CODES): # 20/30 Loop ramps | 28/29 MM on/off ramps | 41/42 Fwy on/off ramps # 21/31 CD roads (collector-distributor geometry) # # Class 3 — Surface Streets (SURFACE_CODES): # 2/3 Principal/Minor Arterial | 4/5 Major/Minor Collector | 6/7 Local/Unpaved # FT=2 links with DIRECTION=1 are excluded (they belong to Class 1 above). # # Filtered out at load time (not present in gdf_links): # 1 Centroid connector (zone-to-network tap, not a physical road) # 37 HOV lane | 38 Managed lane | 39 Managed-lane access point # Removed before any processing so they cannot inflate LinkCount/NeighborCount, # corrupt the pseudonode LinkClassCount check, pollute the direction cascade, # or produce spurious surface-type node classifications. # # Loaded but excluded from the three road classes: # 70/80 Fixed transit | N ranges 10000-19999 and 50000-59999 (station nodes) # Transit geometry is handled separately in the FixedTransit snapping pass. # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── FT_COL = "FT_2027" # DIRECTION=1 flag: handles both numeric 1 and string "1" encodings _oneway = gdf_links["ONEWAY"].astype(str).str.strip() == "1" # Mutually exclusive link masks (LN_2027 > 0 already filtered at load time) # 1. Freeway includes interstates and expressways/principal arterials that have directional travel FWY_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 40}) | ( gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({2, 12, 13, 14, 15}) & _oneway ) # 2. Ramp and Collector-Distributor Roads share the same mask RAMP_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({20, 21, 28, 29, 30, 31, 41, 42}) # 3. Centroid Connectors (FT = 1) CC_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL] == 1 # 4. HOV and Managed Lanes (FT = 37, 38, 39) MANAGED_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({37, 38, 39}) # 5. Remaining Links get assigned as Surface Links SURFACE_LINK_MASK = ~(FWY_LINK_MASK | RAMP_LINK_MASK | CC_LINK_MASK | MANAGED_LINK_MASK) # FW_CODES: FT codes used for the freeway-priority direction cascade. # FT=2+DIRECTION=1 is classified as freeway but excluded here because its # DIRECTION value is a one-way flag, not a cardinal (NB/SB/EB/WB). # Direction matching for those links is deferred to the geometric heading # approach planned for the ramp refactor (Request 3). FW_CODES = {12, 13, 14, 15, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 40} gdf_nodes["Freeway"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[FWY_LINK_MASK]) gdf_nodes["Ramp"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[RAMP_LINK_MASK]) gdf_nodes["Surface"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[SURFACE_LINK_MASK]) gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] = ( nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, {70, 80}) | gdf_nodes["N"].between(10_000, 19_999) # TRAX station nodes | gdf_nodes["N"].between(50_000, 59_999) # FrontRunner station nodes ) flag_cols = ["Freeway", "Ramp", "Surface", "FixedTransit"] print("Flag counts:") print(gdf_nodes[flag_cols].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string()) print( f"\nLinks by class — Freeway: {FWY_LINK_MASK.sum():,} " f"| Ramp: {RAMP_LINK_MASK.sum():,} " f"| Surface: {SURFACE_LINK_MASK.sum():,}" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Flag counts: NodeCount Freeway 1885 Ramp 1634 Surface 10756 FixedTransit 610 Links by class — Freeway: 1,870 | Ramp: 1,344 | Surface: 24,779 ``` ::: ::: ### A2 — Link Count, Neighbor Count, and Direction `count_links` adds `LinkCount` — raw endpoint references per node, used for the distribution chart and diagnostics. `count_neighbors` adds `NeighborCount` — the number of **distinct** neighboring nodes. This is the topologically correct pseudonode test: `LinkCount` breaks on bidirectional roads stored as two directional records (a pseudonode appears 4 times → LinkCount=4, not 2), while NeighborCount is always 2 for a true shape vertex regardless of storage convention. A node is an `is_pseudo` shape vertex if and only if both conditions hold: 1. **NeighborCount == 2** — lies on a single path between two real junctions. 2. **LinkClassCount == 1** — all connected links share the same functional class. A node where a freeway meets a surface street has NeighborCount=2 but LinkClassCount=2, so it is a real class-transition junction and must be snapped. `assign_node_directions` produces two direction columns (see docstring): - `link_directions` — freeway-priority cascade; used in passes (a) and (c). - `fw_directions` — freeway links only, majority-vote; used in pass (b) gore nodes. ::: {#e8e01c10 .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} gdf_nodes = count_links(gdf_nodes, gdf_links) gdf_nodes = count_neighbors(gdf_nodes, gdf_links) gdf_nodes = assign_node_directions(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, freeway_ft_codes=FW_CODES) # Assign one class label per link using the already-defined mutually exclusive masks. _link_class = pd.Series("other", index=gdf_links.index) _link_class[FWY_LINK_MASK] = "freeway" _link_class[RAMP_LINK_MASK] = "ramp" _link_class[SURFACE_LINK_MASK] = "surface" _link_class[gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({70, 80})] = "transit" # For each node, count how many distinct link classes touch it. _stacked_class = pd.concat([ gdf_links[["A"]].assign(cls=_link_class.values).rename(columns={"A": "N"}), gdf_links[["B"]].assign(cls=_link_class.values).rename(columns={"B": "N"}), ], ignore_index=True) _class_counts = _stacked_class.groupby("N")["cls"].nunique() gdf_nodes["LinkClassCount"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(_class_counts).fillna(0).astype(int) # Count distinct FT codes per node. # Stricter than LinkClassCount: nodes where FT changes (e.g. FT=32→33) register # LinkFTCount=2 even though both belong to the freeway class. Used in the future # link true-shape dissolution step to preserve model link boundaries where posted # speed or facility sub-type changes — dissolving those nodes would corrupt TDM impedance. _stacked_ft = pd.concat([ gdf_links[["A", FT_COL]].rename(columns={"A": "N"}), gdf_links[["B", FT_COL]].rename(columns={"B": "N"}), ], ignore_index=True) _ft_counts = _stacked_ft.groupby("N")[FT_COL].nunique() gdf_nodes["LinkFTCount"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(_ft_counts).fillna(0).astype(int) # Pseudonode: shape vertex on a single homogeneous path — no physical snap target. # Uses LinkFTCount (not LinkClassCount): UGRC/UDOT centerlines are segmented at # posted speed limit changes, so a node where FT changes (e.g. FT=32→33) has a # real physical endpoint in the centerline data and must be sent to snapping. # LinkClassCount would incorrectly skip those nodes (both FT codes map to "freeway" # class), causing missed snaps at speed-transition points along freeway mainlines. gdf_nodes["is_pseudo"] = (gdf_nodes["NeighborCount"] == 2) & (gdf_nodes["LinkFTCount"] == 1) print(f"Pseudonodes (NeighborCount==2, single class): {gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'].sum():,}") print(f" of which LinkCount==2: {(gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'] & (gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] == 2)).sum():,}") print(f" of which LinkCount> 2: {(gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'] & (gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] > 2)).sum():,} ← bidirectional road pseudonodes missed by old filter") print(f"Class-transition nodes (NeighborCount==2, multi-class): {((gdf_nodes['NeighborCount'] == 2) & (gdf_nodes['LinkClassCount'] > 1)).sum():,} ← real junctions, must be snapped") print(f"FT-transition nodes now sent to snapping (NeighborCount==2, same class, diff FT): {((gdf_nodes['NeighborCount'] == 2) & (gdf_nodes['LinkClassCount'] == 1) & (gdf_nodes['LinkFTCount'] > 1)).sum():,}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Pseudonodes (NeighborCount==2, single class): 9,003 of which LinkCount==2: 1,544 of which LinkCount> 2: 7,459 ← bidirectional road pseudonodes missed by old filter Class-transition nodes (NeighborCount==2, multi-class): 22 ← real junctions, must be snapped FT-transition nodes now sent to snapping (NeighborCount==2, same class, diff FT): 127 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#2b93d24f .cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Link-count distribution chart link_count_freq = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index() max_val = link_count_freq.max() BASE_COLOR = "#B4B2A9" ACCENT_COLOR = "#1D9E75" PSEUDO_COLOR = "#EF9F27" LABEL_COLOR = "#444441" GRID_COLOR = "#D3D1C7" bar_colors = [ PSEUDO_COLOR if lc == 2 else (ACCENT_COLOR if lc in {4, 6, 8} else BASE_COLOR) for lc in link_count_freq.index ] fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5.5)) fig.patch.set_facecolor("white") ax.set_facecolor("white") ax.yaxis.grid(True, color=GRID_COLOR, linewidth=0.6, zorder=0) ax.set_axisbelow(True) bars = ax.bar(link_count_freq.index, link_count_freq.values, color=bar_colors, width=0.75, zorder=2) for bar in bars: h = bar.get_height() ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, h + max_val * 0.012, f"{int(h):,}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9.5, color=LABEL_COLOR) for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]: ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False) ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR) ax.set_xticks(link_count_freq.index) ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}")) ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=9.5, colors="#888780", length=0) ax.tick_params(axis="x", length=0) ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.18) ax.set_xlabel("Number of links connected to node", fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", labelpad=10) ax.legend( [plt.Rectangle((0,0),1,1,color=c) for c in [ACCENT_COLOR, PSEUDO_COLOR, BASE_COLOR]], ["Typical intersection (4, 6, 8)", "Pseudonode (2)", "Other"], loc="upper right", fontsize=9, frameon=False, labelcolor="#888780", ) fig.text(0.13, 1.01, "Most nodes connect to 4, 6, or 8 links", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", color=LABEL_COLOR, transform=ax.transAxes) fig.text(0.13, 0.94, f"Distribution of {len(gdf_nodes):,} network nodes by link count", fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", transform=ax.transAxes) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](01_node_classification_files/figure-html/cell-13-output-1.png){width=1047 height=537} ::: ::: ### A3 — Node Topology Classification Eight mutually-exclusive `node_type` labels. Transit is evaluated first and takes priority over all road-class combinations. The remaining seven labels derive from the full combinatorics of the (Freeway, Ramp, Surface) flags. HOV/managed-lane nodes (FT 37-39) were filtered at load time, so they contribute no flags; those nodes fall to "surface" and are not snapped. Pseudonodes (LinkCount == 2) are shape vertices with no physical counterpart. ::: {#6ef9776f .cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} no_pseudo = ~gdf_nodes["is_pseudo"] is_fwy_node = gdf_nodes["Freeway"] & no_pseudo is_ramp_node = gdf_nodes["Ramp"] & no_pseudo is_surface_node = gdf_nodes["Surface"] & no_pseudo is_transit_node = gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] & no_pseudo # Full combinatorics of the three link classes → 7 node types. # Transit is evaluated first — station nodes get "transit" regardless of any # road-class links they may also touch. # Most-specific road combinations (all three flags) are evaluated next. gdf_nodes["node_type"] = np.select( [ is_transit_node, # fixed rail station is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & is_surface_node, # fwy+ramp+sf → at-grade interchange is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node, # fwy+ramp → elevated gore is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & is_surface_node, # fwy+sf → at-grade fwy crossing is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node, # fwy only → pure mainline ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & is_surface_node, # ramp+sf → ramp terminal ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node, # ramp only → mid-ramp ], ["transit", "gore_sf", "gore", "fwy_sf", "fwy", "ramp_sf", "ramp"], default="surface", ) print("Node type distribution:") print(gdf_nodes["node_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string()) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Node type distribution: count node_type surface 19948 gore 669 transit 609 ramp_sf 322 ramp 187 fwy_sf 124 fwy 48 gore_sf 14 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#b87c549b .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Optional: inspect node flags and directions gdf_nodes[["N", "LinkCount", "node_type", "link_directions", "fw_directions"]].head(20) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=11} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: {#2781b14a .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} out_path = PATHS["out_node_classified"] out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) gdf_nodes.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG") print(f"Classified nodes saved to: {out_path}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Classified nodes saved to: _data\processed\nodes_classified.gpkg ``` ::: ::: --- ## Part B — Target Processing (Centerline Endpoints) The endpoint layer makes topology and direction assignments explicit and inspectable in QGIS **before** any snapping happens. The full workflow is: B1 filter to active segments → B2 classify segments → B3 extract endpoints → B4 deduplicate → B5 assign directions → B6 assign ep_type → B7 export. ### B1 — Filter to Active Segments ::: {#7103da85 .cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Exclude retired / planned segments and segments that fall outside the three # mutually exclusive classes (DupRoute routing artifacts, Institutional roads, # and records with null/empty DOT_FCLASS that cannot be classified). _status_ok = ~gdf_centerlines["STATUS"].isin(["Retired", "Planned"]) _not_duprt = gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("") != "DupRoute" _fclass_ok = ( gdf_centerlines["DOT_FCLASS"].notna() & ~gdf_centerlines["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Instituitional", ""]) # note: source-data typo ) # Ramp/CD segments may have blank DOT_FCLASS but a valid DOT_RTNAME — keep them. _is_ramp_seg = ( gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("").str.contains("R", na=False) | gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("").str.contains("C", na=False) ) & _not_duprt active_cl = gdf_centerlines[_status_ok & _not_duprt & (_fclass_ok | _is_ramp_seg)].copy() print(f"Active centerlines: {len(active_cl):,} of {len(gdf_centerlines):,}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Active centerlines: 25,268 of 25,358 ``` ::: ::: ### B2 — Classify Segments Three mutually exclusive boolean flags per segment. Primary authority is `DOT_FCLASS` for freeway identification; `DOT_RTNAME` (UDOT LRS) supplements with explicit route numbers and ramp/CD facility characters. `ONEWAY` in (`1`, `2`) elevates divided Principal Arterials into the freeway class (domain CVDomain_OneWay: 0=Two-way, 1=One-way direction of arc, 2=One-way opposite direction of arc). ``` DOT_RTNAME layout (11 characters, zero-padded): [0:4] Route number e.g. "0015" = I-15, "0154" = Bangerter [4] LRS direction "P" = primary (NB/EB), "N" = negative (SB/WB) [5] Facility type " " = mainline, "R" = ramp, "C" = collector-distributor [6:] Sequence/suffix varies ``` These flags flow into every endpoint pool and type-tier decision, making this the **primary place to audit** when snapping results look wrong. ::: {#2723a8db .cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} # ── Controlled-access corridors in the Wasatch Front study area ─────────── # Adding / removing a route here is the primary tuning lever for freeway coverage. # These are the 4-character UDOT route numbers (DOT_RTNAME[0:4]). FREEWAY_ROUTES = { "0015", # Interstate 15 (primary N-S spine) "0067", # Legacy Parkway (limited-access, no ramps) "0080", # Interstate 80 (E-W, SLC to Parley's Canyon) "0084", # Interstate 84 (Ogden corridor) "0089", # US-89 (surface in places — watch for mis-classification) "0092", # Timpanogos Highway (SR-92, American Fork Canyon) "0154", # Bangerter Highway (semi-controlled ring road) "0177", # West Davis Corridor (new build, limited segments) "0201", # SR-201 (West Valley connector) "0215", # Interstate 215 (belt route, SLC) } rtname = active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("") # ── Shared exclusion flags ──────────────────────────────────────────────── # Ramp/CD character check: DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' or '%C%', excluding DupRoute. _has_R_or_C = rtname.str.contains("R", na=False) | rtname.str.contains("C", na=False) _not_duprt = rtname != "DupRoute" _not_ramp_cd = ~_has_R_or_C | ~_not_duprt # True when segment is NOT a ramp/CD # ── Class 1: Freeway Mainline ───────────────────────────────────────────── # Primary: DOT_FCLASS identifies interstate / other freeway geometry. # Secondary: explicit UDOT route number is a controlled-access corridor. # Tertiary: Principal Arterial with ONEWAY in ('1','2') represents a # physically separated directional roadway. # ONEWAY domain (CVDomain_OneWay): 0=Two-way, 1=One-way direction # of arc, 2=One-way opposite direction of arc. # All three require exclusion of ramp/CD facility chars and DupRoute aliases. _is_fclass_fwy = active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Interstate", "Other Freeway"]) _is_named_fwy = rtname.str.slice(0, 4).isin(FREEWAY_ROUTES) _is_dir_pa = ( (active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"] == "Principal Arterial") & active_cl["ONEWAY"].isin(["1", "2"]) ) active_cl["is_freeway"] = ( (_is_fclass_fwy | _is_named_fwy | _is_dir_pa) & ~_has_R_or_C & _not_duprt ) # This enrichment is done **explicitly here** (not hidden inside `snap_nodes`) so the lookup table is inspectable. ::: {#8d7e6e62 .cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Build a flat edge list: (node_N, FT, DIRECTION) — vectorised via pd.concat edges = pd.concat([ gdf_links[["A", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"A": "N"}), gdf_links[["B", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"B": "N"}), ], ignore_index=True) edges["is_freeway_link"] = edges["FT_2027"].isin(FW_CODES) edges["DIRECTION"] = edges["DIRECTION"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper() CARDINAL = {"NB", "SB", "EB", "WB"} # For each interchange-only node, collect cardinals from adjacent freeway links. ic_only_mask = ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node ic_only_Ns = set(gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "N"]) adj_fw = ( edges[edges["N"].isin(ic_only_Ns) & edges["is_freeway_link"] & edges["DIRECTION"].isin(CARDINAL)] .groupby("N")["DIRECTION"] .apply(lambda s: ",".join(sorted(set(s)))) ) gdf_nodes["ic_fw_directions"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(adj_fw).fillna("") resolved = (gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "ic_fw_directions"] != "").sum() total_ic = ic_only_mask.sum() print(f"Interchange-only nodes with freeway-neighbor direction: {resolved:,} of {total_ic:,}") print(f" remaining {total_ic - resolved:,} are direction-agnostic (no adjacent freeway link)") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Interchange-only nodes with freeway-neighbor direction: 6 of 509 remaining 503 are direction-agnostic (no adjacent freeway link) ``` ::: ::: --- ## Part D — Node Snapping Hierarchical execution — first-call-wins. Nodes snapped in an earlier pass are skipped by subsequent passes. ### D1 — Freeway and Interchange Snapping Four passes in topology-priority order: (a) pure freeway, (a2) at-grade freeway crossings, (b) gore/gore_sf, (c) mid-ramp. All include `fwy_sf` in the D1 debug export so at-grade crossing snaps can be verified before running surface snapping. ::: {#05efb016 .cell execution_count=25} ``` {.python .cell-code} # ── Endpoint pools ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # # Pass (a) — fwy nodes → fwy endpoints only. # Strict pool: no ramp or surface endpoints. fwy_sf nodes touch freeway links, so the freeway direction signal is reliable. # Surface endpoints are intentionally excluded from this pool — if no fwy-class # endpoint is within range the node falls through to D2 (surface pass) where # surface endpoints are available as a Tier-1 fallback. ep_fwy_sf = ( pd.concat( [ gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy_sf"], gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy"], gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"], ] ) .drop_duplicates() .copy() ) # _TYPE_TIER rejects ramp_sf for plain gore nodes, so the extension is harmless. ep_gore = ( pd.concat( [ gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"], gdf_ep_unique[ (gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy") & (gdf_ep_unique["fw_route_count"] >= 2) ], gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "ramp_sf"], # for gore_sf nodes ] ) .drop_duplicates() .copy() ) # Pass (c) — ramp nodes → ramp + ramp_sf endpoints. # ramp_sf nodes are excluded: they have surface links and route to D2 where # their at-grade topology is Tier-0. ep_ic_only = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].isin(["ramp", "ramp_sf"])].copy() print(f"Pass (a) pool — fwy: {len(ep_fwy_only):,}") print(f"Pass (a2) pool — fwy_sf + fwy + gore: {len(ep_fwy_sf):,}") print(f"Pass (b) pool — gore + ramp_sf: {len(ep_gore):,}") print(f"Pass (c) pool — ramp + ramp_sf: {len(ep_ic_only):,}") # network-side endpoints of FT_2027 = 1 (centroid connector) links — the taps where zone centroids connect to the road network. Zone centroids themselves (N < 3600) are excluded; only the network attachment nodes (N ≥ 3600) are retained. These nodes are snapped to the nearest physical centerline **vertex** (not segment endpoint) using a simple STRtree nearest-vertex lookup — no direction filtering, no greedy assignment. The result is written as a second layer in `nodes_snapped.gpkg` so Stage 2 can use them as additional centerline split points. ::: {#17134822 .cell execution_count=43} ``` {.python .cell-code} gdf_cc_links = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_network_links"], where="FT_2027 = 1") cc_node_ids = set(gdf_cc_links["A"]) | set(gdf_cc_links["B"]) print(f"Centroid connector links: {len(gdf_cc_links):,} | unique node IDs: {len(cc_node_ids):,}") gdf_cc_nodes = gdf_nodes[ gdf_nodes["N"].isin(cc_node_ids) & (gdf_nodes["N"] >= 3600) ].copy() print(f"CC attachment nodes (N ≥ 3600): {len(gdf_cc_nodes):,}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Centroid connector links: 18,394 | unique node IDs: 10,093 CC attachment nodes (N ≥ 3600): 6,531 ``` ::: ::: ::: {#1566553c .cell execution_count=44} ``` {.python .cell-code} gdf_cc_snapped = snap_cc_nodes(gdf_cc_nodes.to_crs(CRS_UTM), gdf_centerlines) n_resolved = gdf_cc_snapped["snap_resolved"].sum() n_unresolved = len(gdf_cc_snapped) - n_resolved dist = gdf_cc_snapped.loc[gdf_cc_snapped["snap_resolved"], "snap_dist_m"] print(f"Resolved: {n_resolved:,} | Unresolved (> 500 m): {n_unresolved:,}") print(f"Snap distance (m): median={dist.median():.1f} | P90={dist.quantile(0.90):.1f} | max={dist.max():.1f}") gdf_cc_snapped.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(out_path, layer="cc_nodes_snapped", driver="GPKG") print(f"CC attachment nodes saved to: {out_path} (layer: cc_nodes_snapped)") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Resolved: 5,935 | Unresolved (> 500 m): 596 Snap distance (m): median=15.0 | P90=221.5 | max=499.1 ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` D:\GitHub\TDM-GEO-TrueShape\_src\node_utils.py:904: UserWarning: snap_cc_nodes: 596 CC attachment node(s) had no centerline vertex within 500.0 m — x_snap/y_snap left NaN. warnings.warn( ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` CC attachment nodes saved to: _data\processed\nodes_snapped.gpkg (layer: cc_nodes_snapped) ``` ::: :::
